clause.title: Ventilation of a roof space with the primary insulation layer not parallel to the roof plane

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: In climate zones 6, 7 and 8, a roof with the primary insulation layer not parallel to the roof plane must have a roof space that—

ol.li: has a height not less than 18 mm at any point between the primary insulation layer and the underside of the roof or a control layer; and
is located immediately above the primary insulation layer; and
is ventilated to outdoor air, in accordance with Table F8D5.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (1) do not apply to a—

ol.li: roof that is made of insulated sandwich panels; or
roof that is subject to Bushfire Attack Level FZ requirements in accordance with AS 3959.
tiled roof without a control layer.

callout.p: Ventilation openings are to be evenly distributed to avoid creating pockets of stagnant air.
Multi-storey construction does not require the duplication of the longest plan dimension of a roof for the purposes of calculating the ventilation opening requirement.

Openings created by roof cladding profile can help meet ventilation opening requirements. Examples include openings underneath a metal cladding profile or inherent openings in a profiled tile roof, provided these openings are directly connected to the roof void to be ventilated and remain unobstructed.

Typical fasteners used in roofing application that may intrude into the ventilation open area will not cause excessive resistance to airflow. Components such as spacers, battens and the like greater than 18 mm in height are acceptable provided they do not create excessive resistance to airflow. Components such as meshes which may restrict the entry of vermin, ember or corrosive aerosols, or the like may also be used if they facilitate drainage and ventilation as required by this part.

For roof pitches greater than 10 degrees, total low level ventilation openings must be greater than total high level ventilation openings to minimise drawing air from occupied spaces and ensure intake of outdoor air. For roof pitches less than 10 degrees (excluding skillion roofs), ventilation of the roof space is facilitated by cross ventilation at opposing ends. High level ventilation is not necessary.
Explanatory Figure F8D5a is an example of a roof space with low level ventilation.
Explanatory Figure F8D5b is an example on calculating high and low level ventilation openings.

Explanatory Figure F8D5c is an example of when a roof is comprised of one roof type (ceiling not parallel to roof plane) but has multiple pitches e.g. a mansard roof. As the roof at the lower level has a pitch of 40 degrees, the low level and high level ventilation opening requirement of 7,000 mm2/m and 5000 mm2/m would apply. Additionally, mansard roofs with a restricted air space of similar magnitudes to ceilings parallel to the roof plane shall follow the requirements in F8D6.
